content.text: What airline had the highest number of passengers among Russian air carriers in 2020?

messages.content:
<chart_summary> This bar chart displays the passenger count in millions for five different Russian air carriers. The x-axis lists the airline names, and the y-axis represents the passenger count in millions.
<extraction> Aeroflot: 14.6 million passengers; S7 Airlines: 12.3 million passengers; Pobeda: 9.1 million passengers; Rossiya: 5.7 million passengers; Ural Airlines: 5.6 million passengers.
<computation> Comparing the passenger counts: 14.6 (Aeroflot) is greater than 12.3 (S7 Airlines), 9.1 (Pobeda), 5.7 (Rossiya), and 5.6 (Ural Airlines). Therefore, Aeroflot had the highest number of passengers.
<answer> Aeroflot

<chart_summary> The image is a pie chart illustrating the distribution of responses to a question, categorized as 'Yes', 'No', and 'I don't know', with each category represented by a slice proportional to its percentage.
<extraction> The percentages for each category are: Yes = 77%, No = 16%, I don't know = 7%.
<computation> Comparing the percentages: 77% (Yes) is greater than 16% (No) and 7% (I don't know). Therefore, 'Yes' is the dominant category.
<answer> Yes

content.text: What is the color of the pie represented "More"?

messages.content:
<chart_summary> The chart is a pie chart showing the results of a survey asking Indian adults about allowing immigrants into India, with different slices representing the percentage of respondents for various opinions.
<extraction> The pie slice labeled "More" is colored gray.
<computation> The color associated with the "More" category is directly observed.
<answer> gray

content.text: What is the average  Share of individuals who downloaded purchased media online in Great Britain in 2013  by type of media?

messages.content:
<chart_summary>
The chart is a clustered column chart displaying the share of respondents who downloaded purchased media online in Great Britain for different media types in 2013 and 2014. The y-axis represents the share of respondents in percentages, and the x-axis lists three categories of media.
</chart_summary>
<extraction>
Share of respondents for "Films or music" in 2013: 50%
Share of respondents for "Electronic books, magazines, newspapers or e-learning material" in 2013: 52%
Share of respondents for "Computer software, computer and video games or software upgrades" in 2013: 66%
</extraction>
<computation>
To find the average share for 2013, sum the shares for each media type in 2013 and divide by the number of media types.
Sum = 50% + 52% + 66% = 168%
Number of media types = 3
Average = 168% / 3 = 56%
</computation>
<answer>
56
</answer>

content.text: How many times France data Higher than Argentina data?

messages.content:
<chart_summary> This bar chart displays the average rapeseed yields in 1976 for Europe, France, and Argentina, measured in tonnes per hectare. The y-axis lists the regions, and the x-axis represents the yield in tonnes.
<extraction>
France data: 1.93 t
Argentina data: 0.67 t
<computation>
To find out how many times France's data is higher than Argentina's data, divide France's yield by Argentina's yield:
1.93 t / 0.67 t = 2.880597...
Rounding to two decimal places, this is 2.88.
<answer>
2.88

content.text: In which country the difference between blue and green bar is maximum?

messages.content:
<chart_summary> The chart is a horizontal stacked bar chart showing the percentage of people who say different entities (Our country, WHO, EU, China, U.S.) have done a "Bad" (blue bar) or "Good" (green bar) job dealing with the coronavirus outbreak.
<extraction> For "Our country", the "Bad" percentage is 25% and the "Good" percentage is 74%.
For "WHO", the "Bad" percentage is 34% and the "Good" percentage is 64%.
For "EU", the "Bad" percentage is 39% and the "Good" percentage is 57%.
For "China", the "Bad" percentage is 60% and the "Good" percentage is 37%.
For "U.S.", the "Bad" percentage is 84% and the "Good" percentage is 15%.
<computation> The difference between the blue and green bars for "Our country" is |74% - 25%| = 49%.
The difference between the blue and green bars for "WHO" is |64% - 34%| = 30%.
The difference between the blue and green bars for "EU" is |57% - 39%| = 18%.
The difference between the blue and green bars for "China" is |60% - 37%| = 23%.
The difference between the blue and green bars for "U.S." is |84% - 15%| = 69%.
Comparing these differences (49%, 30%, 18%, 23%, 69%), the maximum difference is 69%.
<answer> U.S.

content.text: Which year showed the largest difference in the data points between the two lines

messages.content:
<chart_summary>This is a line chart showing the life expectancy at birth in years for females and males from 2009 to 2019. The x-axis represents the year, and the y-axis represents life expectancy in years.</chart_summary>
<extraction>For 2009: female life expectancy = 74.29 years, male life expectancy = 69.01 years.
For 2010: female life expectancy = 74.74 years, male life expectancy = 69.59 years.
For 2011: female life expectancy = 75.22 years, male life expectancy = 70.15 years.
For 2012: female life expectancy = 75.68 years, male life expectancy = 70.64 years.
For 2013: female life expectancy = 76.13 years, male life expectancy = 71.01 years.
For 2014: female life expectancy = 76.54 years, male life expectancy = 71.27 years.
For 2015: female life expectancy = 76.9 years, male life expectancy = 71.42 years.
For 2016: female life expectancy = 77.2 years, male life expectancy = 71.5 years.
For 2017: female life expectancy = 77.45 years, male life expectancy = 71.53 years.
For 2018: female life expectancy = 77.67 years, male life expectancy = 71.58 years.
For 2019: female life expectancy = 77.84 years, male life expectancy = 71.65 years.</extraction>
<computation>Calculate the difference for each year:
2009: 74.29 - 69.01 = 5.28
2010: 74.74 - 69.59 = 5.15
2011: 75.22 - 70.15 = 5.07
2012: 75.68 - 70.64 = 5.04
2013: 76.13 - 71.01 = 5.12
2014: 76.54 - 71.27 = 5.27
2015: 76.9 - 71.42 = 5.48
2016: 77.2 - 71.5 = 5.70
2017: 77.45 - 71.53 = 5.92
2018: 77.67 - 71.58 = 6.09
2019: 77.84 - 71.65 = 6.19
Comparing all differences (5.28, 5.15, 5.07, 5.04, 5.12, 5.27, 5.48, 5.70, 5.92, 6.09, 6.19), the largest difference is 6.19, which occurred in 2019.</computation>
<answer>2019</answer>

content.text: Which capital is the highest value in the bar graph?

messages.content:
<chart_summary>
The chart is a horizontal bar graph displaying the total wealth by asset group in 2014 US dollars. The y-axis lists different asset groups (Human capital, Produced capital, Natural capital, Net foreign assets), and the x-axis represents their value in trillions of dollars.
</chart_summary>
<extraction>
Human capital: $742.07 trillion
Produced capital: $303.55 trillion
Natural capital: $107.43 trillion
Net foreign assets: $-4.58 trillion
</extraction>
<computation>
Comparing the values:
$742.07 trillion (Human capital)
$303.55 trillion (Produced capital)
$107.43 trillion (Natural capital)
$-4.58 trillion (Net foreign assets)

The highest value among these is $742.07 trillion, which corresponds to Human capital.
</computation>
<answer>
Human capital
</answer>
